name: FLY2K
short_description: A Y2K, cyberspace, butterfly flight sim. Flap using 2 button controls, avoid malware obstacles, boost your speed and break free from the web!
detailed_description: FLY2K is a retro-cyberspace flight sim with deviously simple controls and a need for speed. Pass through gates to gain speed, smash through barriers at super speed to progress and avoid the input killing malware to escape the web and ascend to a higher plane of being!
about_the_game: FLY2K is a retro-cyberspace flight sim with deviously simple controls and a need for speed. Pass through gates to gain speed, smash through barriers at super speed to progress and avoid the input killing malware to escape the web and ascend to a higher plane of being!

reviews.review_text:
Very much enjoying the sense of speed & the music. There's a great overall vapor-wave vibe to the environment, and the dynamic colors & music progression as you go faster and faster are great. 

I'm a handful of runs in, and I can't tell what my "high score" is just from the menus alone. Maybe that's unlocked the further I get, maybe it's something that could potentially be added in an update? 

Either way, I'm stoked on the feeling of getting better at lining up my little butterfly through the rings. Fun times! (Also, this game ran great for me via Crossover on macOS and via GameHub on Android. Woo!)
I got played the demo and picked up the main game. I find myself sucked into playing one more run over and over again trying to get through the second gate. :)
This is a cool game. I like it. You will too.

Really gorgeous, love the visuals. Gameplay is great, but I think stage 2 might be harder than stages 3 and 4! Would love to see more stages, an infinite mode or some kind of unlocks after beating a run. Really cool experience, will probably come back to this one every so often.
Super simple mechanics and concept held up by a transcendental visual style. I wish it had a little more pop here and there, but it's a good way to spend a quick 20-40 minutes!
